$note: Captured snapshot of the COMPARED forecast/intelligence product for the azimuth benchmark (synthesis/benchmark.py). Quoted by product + capture date; NOT surfaced as an azimuth channel and NOT an L1 source. Refresh with pull_benchmark_foils.py.

foil.title: Inflation and rates pressure from Iraq infrastructure fragility state
foil.scenario_short: Iraq faces a 61% probability of increased inflation and rates pressure over the next 30 days, driven by infrastructure fragility feeding energy supply shock with 39% pressure and 57% state confirmation, and reinforced...
foil.perspective_strategic: The rising probability (61%) of inflation and rates pressure in Iraq, linked to infrastructure fragility and energy supply shock (39% pressure, 57% state confirmation), signals a growing economic vulnerability.
foil.perspective_contrarian: The current 75% confidence level leaves room for reversal; a lack of further direct reporting on Iraq's infrastructure issues could prevent the escalation of inflation and rates pressure, potentially stabilizing the situation below the 61% baseline.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Iraq becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 61%.
foil.top_signal: Iraq infrastructure fragility state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through energy supply shock, with 39% pressure and 57% state confirmation

seismic-infrastructure.absence_reason: No product publishes a seismic forecast — earthquakes are not predictable, so there is no forecast/intel alternative to azimuth's observed USGS record. This is the honest inverse: for an observed natural-hazard fact, the sourced record IS the only trustworthy source.
